statement.en: A Framework for Understanding Moving Image Music Concerts: Repertoire, Reception and Recontextualisation
statement.summary: This thesis investigates the phenomenon of moving image music concerts, focussing on the live orchestral presentation of music from film, television, and video games. With orchestras increasingly turning to such concerts to attract new audiences, this research seeks to understand what happens when music originally composed for audiovisual media is recontextualised for concert performance. Drawing on an interdisciplinary approach which includes archival study, interviews, and audience surveys, this study considers how the presentation and reception of moving image music differs when divorced from the source text’s typical mode of consumption. Through a detailed analysis of various concert presentation formats, from more typical modes of orchestral concert presentation (Music Only) to entire

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a 2026-dated humanities thesis on concert reception and recontextualisation—a provisional, empirical study not yet validated by peer review. The claims about audience reception, concert formats, and musical recontextualisation are survey-based and context-dependent, subject to ongoing research iteration. No single truth-value applies; the work contributes evidence to a developing field.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 映像から解放された音楽が、観客の時間的な経験を新しく積分する。
